audienceSections.normal: In questions about nearby objects, you will use [[grammar:مَا هَذَا؟ / مَا هَذِهِ]]؟. Use [[grammar:مَا هَذَا]]؟ for a masculine item and [[grammar:مَا هَذِهِ]]؟ for a feminine item. Match هَذَا or هَذِهِ to the noun in the answer. Do not use the same demonstrative for every noun.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:مَا هَذَا؟ / مَا هَذِهِ]]؟ in questions about nearby objects. Interrogative مَا combines with a gender-agreeing proximal demonstrative in a verbless identification question. هَلْ in the yes–no example is separate supporting question grammar, not another lexical sense.

audienceSections.normal: Use [[grammar:مَا هَذَا]]؟ for a masculine item and [[grammar:مَا هَذِهِ]]؟ for a feminine item. Match هَذَا or هَذِهِ to the noun in the answer. Do not use the same demonstrative for every noun.
audienceSections.expert: Interrogative مَا combines with a gender-agreeing proximal demonstrative in a verbless identification question. هَلْ in the yes–no example is separate supporting question grammar, not another lexical sense.

audienceSections.normal: The Arabic lines are in Modern Standard Arabic. The vowel marks show short vowels and case endings, so read every marked vowel instead of guessing from the bare consonants.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses fully vocalized Modern Standard Arabic. Lexical citation forms follow the established perfect-stem convention for verbs, while the learner text preserves the attested inflected form and case vocalization.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact arabic line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:مَا هَذَا؟ / مَا هَذِهِ]]؟ where it appears.

- مَا هَذَا؟ — What is this?
- هَذَا هَاتِفٌ. — This is a phone.
- مَا هَذِهِ؟ — What is this?
- هَذِهِ حَقِيبَةٌ. — This is a bag.
- هَلْ هَذَا مِفْتَاحٌ؟ — Is this a key?
- نَعَمْ، هَذَا مِفْتَاحٌ. — Yes, this is a key.
breakdown.expert:
